chunks.page_summary: This page provides an overview of software tools and methods for PROFINET integration with Rheonics sensors, covering commissioning, diagnostics, engineering, and programming.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Software for Profinet integration with Rheonics Sensors

Modified on: Sun, 9 Jul, 2023 at 1:00 AM

Several tools exist to help with PROFINET integration on the Rheonics sensors, these tools make setting up the PROFINET connection and configuring the sensor parameters easy. They also allow for real-time sensor data monitoring, making it easier to troubleshoot any issues that may arise. Finally, they provide a secure connection for transmitting data between the sensor and other PROFINET-enabled devices.

TABLE OF CONTENTS

1. Commissioning and diagnostics tools
    1.1. PROFINET Commander
        - Steps to detect Rheonics Sensors when using PROFINET commander
    1.2. PRONETA
        - Steps to detect Rheonics Sensors when using PRONETA
    1.3. Wireshark
        - Steps to detect Rheonics Sensors when using Wireshark
2. Engineering and Programming tools
    2.1. Siemens TIA Portal
    2.2. Codesys
    2.3. Beckhoff TwinCAT 3
3. Integration examples developed by Rheonics

1. Commissioning and diagnostics tools

Commissioning and diagnostic tools enable engineers to identify potential issues in PROFINET networks, such as incorrect configuration, latency, and packet loss. They also allow engineers to check that Rheonics sensors are correctly connected and configured for optimal performance and that the sensor is fully operating in a PROFINET network.
If devices cannot be found with any of these tools contact the Rheonics Support team to check the the sensor configuration.

1.1. PROFINET Commander

The PROFINET commander allows for easy configuration of PROFINET devices and networks without the use of PLC. Windows PC can use it as both a PROFINET IO controller and IO supervisor. It allows the reading and writing of device IO data, parameters, and objects related to data records.
key_facts: PROFINET integration tools simplify the setup of PROFINET connections and configuration of Rheonics sensor parameters.
These tools facilitate real-time sensor data monitoring and streamline troubleshooting of potential issues.
The integration ensures a secure connection for data transmission between Rheonics sensors and PROFINET-enabled devices.
Commissioning and diagnostic tools aid engineers in identifying PROFINET network issues such as incorrect configuration, latency, and packet loss.
These tools also verify that Rheonics sensors are properly connected, configured for optimal performance, and fully operational within a PROFINET network.
PROFINET Commander enables easy configuration of PROFINET devices and networks without requiring a PLC.
A Windows PC can operate as both a PROFINET IO controller and an IO supervisor when using PROFINET Commander.
PROFINET Commander supports the reading and writing of device IO data, parameters, and objects associated with data records.
warnings_notes: If devices cannot be found with any of these tools, contact the Rheonics Support team to check the sensor configuration.

key_facts: The PROFINET Commander software is used for managing and diagnosing devices on a PROFINET network.
The software allows users to select a specific Network Interface Card (NIC) for communication with the PROFINET network.
Connected PROFINET devices, such as 'Switch_1' and 'ET200SP', are displayed in a navigation tree.
The ET200SP device includes modules like IM 155-6 PN ST V1.0, DI 8x24VDC HF V1.0, and DQ 16x24VDC/0.5A ST V1.0.
The PROFINET Controller's network configuration, including IP address, subnet mask, and router, can be viewed.
The software logs important events, such as NIC selection, file import success, controller operational status, and device online status.
To identify Rheonics Sensors, users must click the 'Browser' button to open the DCP (Discovery and Configuration Protocol) function window.
The PROFINET Commander software can show diagnostic information for connected devices, including channel-specific errors.

key_facts: The PROFINET Commander software allows users to browse and configure PROFINET devices on a network.
The 'Browse' function is used to initiate the detection of PROFINET devices.
The 'DCP Identify All' button within the PN Browser window discovers all PROFINET devices on the network.
The software lists detected Network Interface Controllers (NICs) and allows selection from a dropdown list.
Once devices are identified, PROFINET settings like IP Address, Subnet Mask, and Router can be configured.
The software provides options to write device names temporarily or reset devices to factory mode.
The log displays information and alarms, including detected NICs and prompts to select a NIC.
diagram_labels: By clicking the DCP Identify button. All PROFINET devices on the network will be found.

1.2. PRONETA

PRONETA is a PC-based commissioning and diagnostics tool that allows checking distributed I/Os - without a PLC. The free Basic version users get a fast, intuitive graphical user interface that offers tools for commissioning, diagnostics, and configuring their PROFINET network. As soon as the software is running and connected to a PROFINET network, it would detect all Rheonics Sensors with PROFINET communication in the network. It is also possible to download the GSDML files to easily access to device description.

key_facts: PN Browser is a tool for PROFINET devices that allows identification, IP parameter configuration, and device name setting.
PRONETA is a PC-based commissioning and diagnostics tool for PROFINET networks, capable of functioning without a PLC.
PRONETA offers an intuitive graphical user interface for commissioning, diagnostics, and configuring PROFINET networks.
PRONETA automatically detects Rheonics Sensors with PROFINET communication when connected to the network.
PRONETA allows downloading GSDML files for easy access to device descriptions.
Rheonics sensors RH B09-0000 SRD and RH B11-0103 SRV are listed as PROFINET-enabled devices with specific MAC addresses and IP configurations.
The Settings button within PRONETA provides access to network configuration and GSDML file installation options.
diagram_labels: Figure 5. DCP function all PROFINET devices in the networks.
Figure 6. Accessing device information through PRONETA Software.[2]

Select correct NIC and click DCP Identify All button to continue.

1.3. Wireshark
Wireshark is a free and open-source packet analyzer. It is used for network troubleshooting, analysis, software, and communications protocol development. Wireshark is capable of capturing traffic from a variety of networks, including Ethernet, WiFi, Bluetooth, and USB. It can also decode the protocols of many networking protocols and display the information in a human-readable format. Wireshark is widely used for network analysis and security auditing.

Figure 14. Wireshark trace of PROFINET network composed of Rheonics Sensors.[3]
Wireshark also provides useful data like PROFINET alarms that are helpful for system integrators to debug possible failures on the network.

Wireshark Trace (partial view):
No. | Time       | Source          | Destination    | Protocol | Length | Info
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

31  | 02:26:55.3 | Rheonics_03:a2  | Siemens1_3c:7a:b0 | PNIO     | 60     | 60 RTCI, ID:0x8001, Len: 40, Cycle:32768 (Valid,Primary,Ok,Run)
32  | 02:26:55.8 | Siemens1_3c:7a:b0 | Rheonics_03:a2   | PNIO     | 60     | 60 RTCI, ID:0x8001, Len: 40, Cycle:49152 (Valid,Primary,Ok,Run)
33  | 02:26:56.3 | Rheonics_03:a2  | Siemens1_3c:7a:b0 | PNIO     | 60     | 60 RTCI, ID:0x8001, Len: 40, Cycle:0 (Valid,Primary,Ok,Run)
34  | 02:26:56.3 | Rheonics_03:a2  | www.rheonics.com | LLDP     | 174    | 174 LA/RH B11-0103 SRV

Steps to detect Rheonics Sensors when using Wireshark:
1. Open Wireshark and select the NIC (Network Interface Card) that is used for the PROFINET network.
key_facts: PRONETA software is used to visualize PROFINET network topology.
Wireshark is a free and open-source packet analyzer employed for network troubleshooting, analysis, and communications protocol development.
Wireshark supports capturing traffic from various networks including Ethernet, WiFi, Bluetooth, and USB.
Wireshark can decode many networking protocols and present information in a human-readable format, making it widely used for network analysis and security auditing.
Wireshark provides PROFINET alarms that assist system integrators in debugging potential network failures, particularly involving Rheonics Sensors.

2. Engineering and Programming tools
key_facts: The document instructs users on how to capture network packets using Wireshark.
Users must select the correct Network Interface Controller (NIC) to capture PROFINET network traffic.
Once the NIC is selected, all network packets will be displayed in the Wireshark interface.
Wireshark display filters, such as 'pn_io', can be used to isolate and view only PROFINET packets.
A list of PROFINET display filters for Wireshark is available online at 'https://www.wireshark.org/docs/dfref/p/pn_io.html'.
Rheonics devices (e.g., 'Rheonics_03:a2') are shown participating in PROFINET communication in the packet captures.
The document is part of a larger section titled 'Engineering and Programming tools'.

chunks.page_summary: This page describes the integration of Rheonics devices and sensors with Beckhoff TwinCAT 3 and other industrial automation software, providing context and listing relevant software tools.
chunks.extracted_text:
2.3. Beckhoff TwinCAT 3

The TwinCAT software system turns almost any PC-based system into a real-time controller with multiple PLC, NC, CNC, and/or robotics runtime systems. It is easy to use and provides a cost-effective way to automate processes. TwinCAT offers advanced features such as distributed I/O systems.

Figure 21. TwinCAT 3 User interface.

Integration of Rheonics devices using Beckhoff TwinCAT 3 is described in detail in the following article: PROFINET integration of Rheonics devices using TwinCAT.

3. Integration examples developed by Rheonics

Examples of a quick integration of Rheonics Sensors with some of the most popular industrial PLC controllers and the associated software are explained in a support article that can be found here: (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/81000195693) Integration with Rheonics Sensors

These examples demonstrate the ease with which Rheonics Sensor can be integrated with existing controller and software solutions.

Software tools discussed in this article are from the following vendors:

[1] PROFINET Commander (https://profinetcommander.com/)
[2] PRONETA (https://www.siemens.com/global/en/products/automation/industrial-communication/profinet/proneta.html#:~:text=PRONETA%20Basic%20is%20a%20free,documentation%20of%20a%20plant%27s%20wiring.)
[3] Wireshark · Go Deep (https://www.wireshark.org/)
[4] TIA Portal (https://www.siemens.com/global/en/products/automation/industry-software/automation-software/tia-portal.html)
[5] CODESYS Group (https://www.codesys.com/)
[6] TwinCAT | Automation software (https://www.beckhoff.com/en-en/products/automation/twincat/?pk_campaign=AdWords-AdWordsSearchAutomation_dynamic_EN&pk_kwd=)
key_facts: Beckhoff TwinCAT 3 software converts PC-based systems into real-time controllers for PLC, NC, CNC, and robotics runtime systems.
TwinCAT 3 is designed for ease of use, cost-effective automation, and includes advanced features like distributed I/O systems.
Detailed integration steps for Rheonics devices with Beckhoff TwinCAT 3 are provided in a dedicated article titled "PROFINET integration of Rheonics devices using TwinCAT".
Rheonics offers examples demonstrating the integration of its sensors with popular industrial PLC controllers and their associated software.
A support article (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/81000195693) details the integration of Rheonics Sensors.
The integration examples showcase the simple integration process of Rheonics Sensors with current controller and software setups.
Software tools relevant to the integration discussions include PROFINET Commander, PRONETA, Wireshark, TIA Portal, CODESYS Group, and TwinCAT Automation software.
